authoritative_sources.title: 25 CFR 543.23 Audit and accounting
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal regulation is the primary public baseline for tribal gaming audit and accounting controls, internal-audit structure, documentation, and annual agreed-upon-procedures expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: 25 CFR 543.24 Auditing revenue
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal regulation is the primary public rule for revenue-audit independence, documentation, operational areas, and minimum reconciliation procedures in tribal gaming.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Daily and periodic revenue-audit evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Auditing Revenue 25 CFR 543.24 Toolkit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The NIGC training toolkit is an official regulator-issued workflow reference that translates 25 CFR 543.24 into concrete testing steps, evidence expectations, and variance handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards Race and Sports Version 9
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada's MICS are authoritative public operating standards for one of the most mature U.S. gaming jurisdictions and directly define sportsbook and race-book records, reconciliations, and tax-return support expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Wagering and sportsbook revenue support review
used_for: daily account wagering detail and summary reports

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards Slots Version 9
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada slot MICS are authoritative for slot meter, jackpot, payout, drop, count, and system-controlled evidence expectations that materially change gaming revenue-audit handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Slot intake and posting support validation

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards Table Games Version 9
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada table-games MICS are authoritative for soft-drop, count, marker, tournament, progressive, and table-game accountability records that shape revenue-audit evidence requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: CPA MICS Compliance Checklists and Guidelines - Internal Audit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official NGCB checklist is a direct public audit-control reference for testing internal-audit utilization, evidence retention, follow-up, and MICS compliance support.

authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69L Taxes
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: New Jersey's Chapter 69L is an authoritative public source for gross-revenue tax, Internet gaming gross-revenue tax, monthly gross-revenue reporting, record examination, and certification boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69D Casino Rules
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: New Jersey's Chapter 69D is an authoritative public operating and control standard for slot monitoring, jackpot and hopper records, internal controls, and the accounting department's revenue-verification responsibilities.

workflow_stages.name: Jurisdiction and control-baseline intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify the jurisdiction, property type, game vertical, and approved internal-control baseline that governs the case before evaluating the financial outcome.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Gaming source-record and posting support review
workflow_stages.description: Validate that drop, count, meter, payout, ticket, wagering-account, cage, and promotional records support the requested posting, gross-revenue summary, or adjustment handling.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation and variance investigation
workflow_stages.description: Compare gaming-system, cage, accounting, and reporting records to identify matched items, unexplained variances, unsupported adjustments, and follow-up owners.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Gross-revenue reporting support and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed support for gross-revenue reporting, tax-return tie-outs, and exception packets without certifying the filing or journal outcome.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when the work requires gaming-compliance interpretation, tax certification, accounting signoff, control redesign, surveillance or IT authority, or unresolved jurisdictional conflict.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: slot meter recaps and jackpot payout slips
hopper fill, credit payout, and progressive logs
slot, table-game, and soft-drop count sheets
fill slips, credit slips, marker documents, and tournament forms
race and sports wagering tickets, wagering-account detail reports, and summary reports
cage accountability records, voucher redemption detail, and patron-deposit records
gross-revenue reports, tax-return support schedules, and revenue-audit workpapers
approved internal controls, TICS or SICS where applicable, and internal-audit follow-up packets
player-tracking exception reports and promotional payout support
systems_of_record: interactive gaming systems and wagering-account platforms when in scope
cage, vault, and kiosk accountability systems
general ledger and financial reporting systems
document repositories for approved internal controls and audit workpapers
internal-audit tracking and exception management systems

allowed: Assess whether visible gaming source records support a posting, gross-revenue support package, reconciliation note, or exception escalation.
Summarize matched and unmatched evidence, missing prerequisites, and the next responsible owner.
Separate distinct game verticals or jurisdiction paths when the public baseline clearly requires different evidence or controls.
escalate: The applicable jurisdiction, approved internal controls, TICS, or SICS are missing or conflict with the public baseline.
Gross-revenue support does not reconcile to the general ledger, wagering recap, or tax-return support schedule.
A variance or override request requires gaming compliance, tax, accounting, surveillance, cage, sportsbook, or legal authority.
Interactive gaming, mobile wagering, or remote-access evidence changes the control expectations and the tenant-specific control map is unavailable.
refuse: Requests to alter meter, count, ticket, wagering-account, payout, or cage facts without source evidence.
Requests to certify tax filings, approve journal entries, redesign internal controls, or provide legal or regulatory interpretation.
Requests to treat the gaming industry label as cosmetic and skip jurisdiction, game-vertical, or approved-control context.

domain_failure_modes: A gaming result is treated as supportable even though slot, cage, or wagering records do not materially agree.
A generic finance adjustment is applied without recognizing jurisdiction-specific gaming-control requirements or approved internal controls.
Unexplained variances are suppressed to force property reporting or tax support to tie.
The lane drifts into tax certification, journal-entry approval, regulatory interpretation, or control-design authority.
Interactive, sportsbook, slot, and table-game workflows are flattened into one evidence model even though the governing records differ materially.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific gaming controls outside the public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public tribal, Nevada, and New Jersey sources establish the baseline used here, but a tenant may operate under a different regulator or under property-specific approved internal controls, TICS, or SICS. The lane therefore requires retrieval of the governing jurisdiction and approved control set before decisive handling.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Interactive gaming and mobile wagering control variation
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Interactive gaming, mobile wagering, and wagering-account controls vary materially by jurisdiction and platform. The package therefore treats these paths as in-scope only when the tenant provides the approved control path, platform logs, and system-of-record precedence for the variant in question.

notes: The public baseline establishes gaming-specific evidence expectations, but tenant retrieval is still required for jurisdiction mapping, approved internal controls, and local reporting ownership.
The specialist is intentionally structured as operator-side revenue-audit handling and must not imply regulator, CPA, tax-signoff, or accounting-close authority.
